pageTitle: All cities with electrical work cost data | Woogoro
metaDescription: Electrical work cost data by city and state. Panel amperage, EV-charger demand, and NEC code-year adoption move the per-circuit total more than labor rate alone.
ogTitle: Electrical work cost by city | Woogoro
ogDescription: Electrical pricing by city. Panel demand, EV-charger circuits, and rolling NEC adoption shift per-circuit numbers.
schemaDescription: Directory of U.S. cities with 2026 electrical work cost data, organized by state, including dominant panel size and code-year context per metro.
intro: Electrical work pricing moves with three slow forces — panel amperage demand (200A is the new floor; 100A is a grandfathered nightmare on many older homes), EV-charging circuit density, and the rolling 2020/2023 NEC adoption of AFCI/GFCI on previously-exempt circuits. A panel upgrade in a 1960s San Diego tract sits around 70% of the same job in a 1920s Boston three-decker because the meter location, service-drop length, and inspection backlog are categorically different. Code-adoption lag also matters: counties still on 2017 NEC don't yet require dual-function AFCI/GFCI breakers on bedroom outlets, which knocks $30–50 per circuit off the bill. Per-city pages list the local utility, dominant panel size in the housing stock, NEC code year in force, and inspection lead-time you can plan against.
